statement.markdown: Given a deque with elements, implement a function to shift all elements to the right by a given positions. For example, if the deque has elements [1, 2, 3, 4] and we want to shift by 2 positions, the deque should become [3, 4, 1, 2]. The function should handle edge cases such as shifting by the length of the deque, shifting by a negative number, or an empty deque.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a list of integers representing the deque elements, and an integer representing the positions to shift.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be a list of integers representing the deque elements after shifting.
statement.notes: The function should modify the input deque in-place, without allocating any additional memory.

hints.text: Consider using a temporary deque to store elements before shifting.

editorial.title: Queue Shift Editorial
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to use a temporary deque to store elements before shifting.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves shifting each element individually.
editorial.betterSolution: Better solution uses a temporary deque to store elements before shifting.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution uses a temporary deque to store elements before shifting, with a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by checking the cases where n is 0, 1, and when there are no shifts or shifts by 1.
